Novak: Russia Considers Complete Ban on Diesel Exports
headlines.summary: Russia is considering a full ban on diesel exports as authorities scramble to stabilize the domestic fuel market amid refinery disruptions, rising prices, and supply shortages linked to Ukrainian attacks on energy infrastructure. headlines.title: Iran Moves to Tap Key Asian Markets as U.S. Waives Oil Sanctions
headlines.summary: Iran is pitching its oil to Asian buyers outside China, contacting India, South Korea, and Japan, as the United States issued a temporary two-month waiver allowing Iranian oil sales, including in U.S. dollars, until August 21. headlines.title: UK Has Enough Electricity for Winter Despite Energy Market Turmoil

headlines.title: Oil Prices Continue to Slide as Peace Talks Progress
headlines.summary: Crude oil prices extended their decline today, with Brent crude sliding to $77.51 per barrel at the time of writing, and West Texas Intermediate at $73.62 per barrel, on reports that the United States and Iran were making progress in their peace talks. headlines.title: How Many Barrels of Oil Do AI Data Centers Consume on a Daily Basis?
headlines.summary: Back in 2017, we did the math to figure out how much oil it takes to mine a single Bitcoin. The answer then was about 20 barrels of oil equivalent per coin. headlines.title: Half-Open, Half-Closed Strait of Hormuz Baffles Oil Markets
headlines.summary: Oil prices have continued to whipsaw since the U.S. and Iran announced last week signed a deal to make a deal as traffic through the Strait of Hormuz is anything but straight and not expected to normalize within days.
